copyright_note: compact paraphrase from internal questionnaire; no long respondent text stored
principle: Retail sales experts use a short initial scan of observable cues to form a tentative, revisable hypothesis about budget, intent strength, use case, and decision-maker.

copyright_note: compact paraphrase from internal questionnaire; no long respondent text stored
principle: Before recommending a specific liquor product, experts first ask for use case such as gifting, business reception, self-use, collection, or family banquet, because the same product cue has different value under different missions.

copyright_note: compact paraphrase from internal questionnaire; no long respondent text stored
principle: Repeated product handling, two-product comparison, phone checking, companion discussion, and stepping back should be treated as hesitation or comparison cues that call for clarification rather than immediate hard closing.

copyright_note: compact paraphrase from internal questionnaire; no long respondent text stored
principle: For price objections, experts anchor value in authenticity, traceability, and service assurance instead of competing only on lower price.

copyright_note: compact paraphrase from internal questionnaire; no long respondent text stored
principle: When customers compare competitors, experts acknowledge the competitor first and then differentiate usage scenario and brand recognition; directly insulting competitors is treated as taboo.

copyright_note: compact paraphrase from internal questionnaire; no long respondent text stored
principle: Authenticity doubts are best handled with visible, verifiable proof such as traceability, third-party inspection, anti-tamper packaging, and in-store demonstration rather than verbal assurance alone.

copyright_note: compact paraphrase from internal questionnaire; no long respondent text stored
principle: Upsell should be tied to a higher-spec occasion or fit requirement; if the customer shows discomfort, the salesperson should stop the upsell and return to the requested product.

copyright_note: compact paraphrase from internal questionnaire; no long respondent text stored
principle: Questions about payment, invoice, delivery, concrete price, and repeated product details are practical closing signals that the salesperson may move from explanation to transaction support.

copyright_note: compact paraphrase from internal questionnaire; no long respondent text stored
principle: In premium liquor retail, physical environment and hardware can function as nonverbal trust evidence by making authenticity, presentation quality, and appraisal capability visible.

copyright_note: compact paraphrase from internal questionnaire; no long respondent text stored
principle: Expert respondents see customer intent as context-dependent and caution that fixed scripts cannot cover every situational variation; AI guidance should preserve uncertainty and ask clarifying questions when evidence is incomplete.
